reasoning:
To resolve YES, Maxime Vachier-Lagrave's rating on the Wikipedia page must be at least 1% higher than his baseline on Nov 23, 2025. Based on the Wikipedia archive, his rating on Nov 22/23 is 2740. A 1% increase requires an Elo of at least 2767.4 (so 2768+).
1) Nov 30, 2025: FIDE publishes official rating lists on the 1st of each month. Therefore, the November 30 Wikipedia page will almost certainly display the exact same November 1 ratings, making it impossible to register an increase.
2) Dec 23, 2025: This will reflect the December list. Gaining 28 points in a single month is phenomenally difficult for a 2740-rated player, requiring an unprecedented streak of over-performances (scoring around 7-8 wins above expected).
3) Feb 21, 2026: This will reflect the February list (accounting for games in Nov, Dec, Jan). While there is slightly more time to string together excellent tournament performances, climbing nearly 30 rating points for a player who has established himself in the 2730-2750 range for years is extremely unlikely. Base rates for such large leaps among top-20 players are negligible.
